audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra skin/scale affliction diagnostic text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for tzaraat/scale disease diagnostics, priestly inspection, quarantine, clean/unclean declarations, garments, and outside-camp status. Strong anti-stigma safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,

When a human has in skin of flesh a swelling, scab, or bright spot, and it becomes in skin of flesh an affliction of scale-disease/tzaraat, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.

The priest shall look at the affliction in skin of flesh; if hair in affliction has turned white and appearance of affliction is deeper than skin of flesh, it is affliction of scale-disease. Priest shall look and declare him unclean.
If bright spot is white in skin of flesh and its appearance is not deeper than skin, and its hair has not turned white, priest shall shut up affliction seven days.
Priest shall look at him on seventh day; if affliction stayed in his eyes and affliction has not spread in skin, priest shall shut him up seven days a second time.
Priest shall look at him on seventh day a second time; if affliction has faded and has not spread in skin, priest shall declare him clean; it is scab. He shall wash garments and be clean.
But if scab spreads in skin after he showed himself to priest for cleansing, he shall show himself to priest a second time.
Priest shall look, and if scab has spread in skin, priest shall declare him unclean; it is scale-disease.
When affliction of scale-disease is in a human, he shall be brought to priest.
Priest shall look, and if there is white swelling in skin, and it has turned hair white, and living raw flesh in swelling,
it is chronic scale-disease in skin of flesh, and priest shall declare him unclean; he shall not shut him up, for he is unclean.
If scale-disease breaks out in skin, and scale-disease covers all skin of afflicted one from head to feet, everywhere eyes of priest see,
then priest shall look, and if scale-disease covers all his flesh, he shall declare afflicted one clean; all of it has turned white; he is clean.
But on day raw flesh appears in him, he shall be unclean.
Priest shall look at raw flesh and declare him unclean; raw flesh is unclean; it is scale-disease.
Or if raw flesh turns again and becomes white, he shall come to priest.
Priest shall look at him, and if affliction turned white, priest shall declare afflicted one clean; he is clean.
When flesh has in its skin a boil and it is healed,
and in place of boil there is white swelling or reddish-white bright spot, it shall be shown to priest.
Priest shall look, and if its appearance is lower than skin and its hair turned white, priest shall declare him unclean; it is affliction of scale-disease broken out in boil.
But if priest looks, and there is no white hair in it and it is not lower than skin and it has faded, priest shall shut him up seven days.
If spreading, it spreads in skin, priest shall declare him unclean; it is affliction.
But if bright spot remains in its place and has not spread, it is scar of boil; priest shall declare him clean.
Or when flesh has in its skin burn from fire, and living part of burn becomes reddish-white or white bright spot,

priest shall look at it; if hair in bright spot turned white and its appearance is deeper than skin, it is scale-disease broken out in burn; priest shall declare him unclean; it is affliction of scale-disease.
But if priest looks, and there is no white hair in bright spot and it is not lower than skin and has faded, priest shall shut him up seven days.
Priest shall look at him on seventh day; if spreading, it spreads in skin, priest shall declare him unclean; it is affliction of scale-disease.
But if bright spot remains in its place, has not spread in skin, and has faded, it is swelling of burn; priest shall declare him clean, for it is scar of burn.
When man or woman has affliction on head or beard,
priest shall look at affliction; if its appearance is deeper than skin and in it thin yellow hair, priest shall declare him unclean; it is scall, scale-disease of head or beard.
If priest looks at affliction of scall, and its appearance is not deeper than skin and there is no black hair in it, priest shall shut up one with affliction of scall seven days.
Priest shall look at affliction on seventh day; if scall has not spread and there is no yellow hair in it, and appearance of scall is not deeper than skin,
then he shall shave himself, but scall he shall not shave; priest shall shut up one with scall seven days a second time.
Priest shall look at scall on seventh day; if scall has not spread in skin and its appearance is not deeper than skin, priest shall declare him clean; he shall wash garments and be clean.
But if spreading, scall spreads in skin after his cleansing,
priest shall look at him, and if scall has spread in skin, priest need not seek yellow hair; he is unclean.
But if in his eyes scall has stayed, and black hair has grown in it, scall is healed; he is clean, and priest shall declare him clean.
When a man or woman has in skin of flesh bright spots, white bright spots,
priest shall look, and if in skin of flesh bright spots are dull white, it is rash broken out in skin; he is clean.
When a man loses hair from his head, he is bald; he is clean.
If from edge of his face he loses hair, he is forehead-bald; he is clean.
But if on bald head or bald forehead there is reddish-white affliction, it is scale-disease breaking out on bald head or bald forehead.
Priest shall look at him, and if swelling of affliction is reddish-white on bald head or bald forehead, like appearance of scale-disease in skin of flesh,
he is a man with scale-disease; he is unclean. Unclean, priest shall declare him; his affliction is on his head.
The one afflicted with scale-disease, in whom affliction is, his garments shall be torn, hair of his head loosened, he shall cover mustache/lip, and cry, Unclean, unclean.
All days that affliction is in him he shall be unclean; unclean he is. Alone he shall dwell; outside camp shall be his dwelling.
When garment has affliction of scale-disease in it, in wool garment or linen garment,
in warp or woof of linen or wool, or in skin or any work of skin,
if affliction is greenish or reddish in garment or skin, in warp or woof or any vessel of skin, it is affliction of scale-disease and shall be shown to priest.
Priest shall look at affliction and shut up afflicted thing seven days.
He shall look at affliction on seventh day. If affliction has spread in garment, in warp or woof, or in skin for any work that is made of skin, affliction is malignant scale-disease; it is unclean.
He shall burn garment, warp or woof in wool or linen, or any vessel of skin in which affliction is, for it is malignant scale-disease; in fire it shall be burned.
But if priest looks, and affliction has not spread in garment, warp or woof, or any vessel of skin,
priest shall command that they wash what has affliction, and he shall shut it up seven days a second time.

Priest shall look after affliction is washed; if affliction has not changed its appearance and affliction has not spread, it is unclean; in fire you shall burn it, whether decay is on bare side or front.
But if priest looks, and affliction has faded after washing it, he shall tear it from garment or skin, from warp or woof.
If it appears again in garment, warp or woof, or any vessel of skin, it is outbreak; in fire you shall burn what has affliction.
Garment, warp or woof, or any vessel of skin that you wash and affliction turns aside from them shall be washed a second time and be clean.
This is Torah/instruction of affliction of scale-disease in garment of wool or linen, or warp or woof, or any vessel of skin, to declare it clean or to declare it unclean.

translation_notes: Tzaraat is not modern leprosy/Hansen's disease by default; render as scale-disease/skin affliction pending lexical audit.
Priest functions diagnostically/ritually, not as moral accuser.
Quarantine and outside-camp status are ritual/community protection measures; do not use for stigma or dehumanization.
Many conditions are declared clean; the chapter carefully distinguishes and reinspects rather than assuming uncleanness.
Garment affliction shows category is broader than human disease.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 13 to stigmatize illness, skin conditions, disability, baldness, infection, or people outside communal space. This is ancient ritual diagnosis and quarantine language, not a statement of human worth or spiritual contamination.
